name: Quest Progression — Milestone Route
description: Prerequisite route for Quest Progression, segmented into milestone episodes by the progression-router planner. Each phase closes on a quest/unlock capstone rather than an arbitrary skill number.

steps.detail: Lumbridge Cook needs ingredients for the Duke's birthday party. Starter quest, no reqs.

hints.note: start: agree to help with the Duke's birthday cake
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Unlocks Fossil Island. Required to mine volcanic ash for ultracompost.

hints.note: start: offer to help finish the barge

steps.detail: Unlocks Morytania region access. Required before Barrows and Slayer tasks there.

hints.note: start: ask about the temple to Saradomin
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Unlocks the Mort Myre Swamp and nature grotto. Required for Fairytale I - Growing Pains.

hints.note: start: ask about Filliman Tarlock
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Required to train Herblore. Short quest, no stat requirements.

mapMarkers.label: Kaqemeex, stone circle N of Taverley (start)

hints.note: start option
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.instruction: Complete Tale of the Righteous (Zeah intro)
steps.detail: One of several quests enabling full Zeah access and CoX entry.

hints.note: start: ask about the expedition
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Track down a crop-raiding beast for farmer Sarah at the Falador farm: follow a trail of stolen vegetables to a hole near Draynor Manor, investigate a skeleton underground, get reinforced goggles from Spria, then kill the level-37 Sourhog and bring its foot back as proof.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Requires the ability to defeat level 45+ enemies (no formal skill/quest gate). Instanced combat gauntlet down a rift near the Digsite: fill a rage bar killing angry unicorns/bears/rats/goblins with themed weapons in room 1, hunt 5-6 fear reapers hiding in holes in room 2, tell 6 confusion beasts apart from their fakes (fakes vanish after 8 hits, give free combat xp) in room 3, kill hopeless creatures 3 times each in room 4, then defeat all 3 heads of Tolna to finish. Progress resets on logout mid-room; re-entering the rift post-quest also completes a Varrock Medium diary task.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: 944 Construction xp splits as 400 flat quest reward + 544 earned in-quest from actually building the 6 furniture pieces (carpet, 2 stools, 2 tables, chair, waxwood bed). The bed needs 3 waxwood planks specifically: search kitchen crates for 3 waxwood logs and take them to the Varrock sawmill operator, who mills them free for this quest. This is a straightforward first-Construction-unlock miniquest (Novice difficulty, no combat), primarily useful for its free POH grant.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Novice quest, opens the troll questline. Talk to Denulth about the troll raids on Burthorpe; ask Eohric on the 1st floor of the castle then Harold at the Burthorpe bar about the missing guard. Give Harold an Asgarnian ale (a Blurberry special auto-wins the dice bet, otherwise gamble over 60gp repeatedly until he goes bankrupt) for an IOU note with a combination; use the combination on the 5 coloured balls at the castle to unlock a door. Talk to Saba in the cave north-west of Burthorpe about another route up the plateau; talk to Tenzing, then Dunstan for a certificate, and exchange the certificate + climbing boots + iron bar for spiked boots. Give Tenzing 10 unnoted bread + 10 unnoted trout + the spiked boots for a map, walk the back route around the mountain (ignore the Death Plateau warning message on the main path), and talk to Denulth with the combination to finish. Required for: Troll Stronghold.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements. Doric wants an errand run for anvil access: buy or mine (with the bronze pickaxe he gives you) 2 iron ore + 4 copper ore + 6 clay, all un-noted, from the Dwarven Mine south of Ice Mountain just east of the start point, then hand them to Doric. One of the short prerequisite quests feeding into Devious Minds and the Falador diary.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Classic quest, gates the Rune platebody and a huge amount of mid-game content. Get Melzar's, Thalzar's, and Lozar's map pieces to Crandor in any order: Thalzar's requires using silk+lobster pot+unfired bowl+wizard's mind bomb on a magic door in the Dwarven Mine; Melzar's requires clearing his eponymous maze in Rimmington, killing a sequence of monsters (zombie rat, ghost, skeleton, zombies, Melzar the Mad, then a level-82 lesser demon) to collect keys; Lozar's is bought from or telegrabbed off Wormbrain in Port Sarim's jail. Combine the three pieces, buy/repair a boat with 3 planks+90 steel nails+2,000 coins from Klarense, sail to Crandor via Ned in Draynor Village, and kill the level-83 dragon Elvarg with an anti-dragon shield equipped (antifire potion recommended). Report back to Oziach in Edgeville to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Short novice quest that unlocks the dwarf multicannon, one of the most impactful early combat-XP tools in the game. Repair 6 bent railings on the west/south fence for Lawgof with a hammer, retrieve dwarf remains from a tower, then find the goblin Lollk hiding in a crate deep in a cave south-east of the Fishing Guild. Reassemble a broken multicannon in the correct tool order (hook on spring, pliers on safety switch, tooth tool on gear), then travel to the Dwarven Black Guard camp to get Nulodion's notes and a cannonball mould before returning to Lawgof to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Third quest in the Mahjarrat series. Lady Servil's family has been kidnapped by General Khazard to fight in his arena. Get Khazard armour from a chest in a house south of Ardougne Monastery and wear it to be let into the prison. Free Sammy Servil after buying Khali brew (5 coins) from the Khazard barman to get the Head Guard drunk and steal his cell keys. Fight through a scripted sequence in the arena: level 63 Khazard ogre, level 44 Khazard scorpion, level 137 Bouncer (hellhound) -- all safespottable behind a skeleton, or use Protect from Melee. The optional level 142 General Khazard fight can be skipped via the door's quick-escape. Return to Lady Servil to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements. Talk to Gertrude, feed a seasoned sardine and milk to her lost cat found up a ladder at the Lumber Yard NE of Varrock (chopping the dying tree there also ticks an easy Varrock diary task), then search crates below for Fluffs' kitten and reunite them before returning to Gertrude for a starter kitten. Feeds into Ratcatchers and other cat-line quests.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No skill/quest requirements, no combat. Goblins are debating a new armour colour. Collect 3 sets of goblin mail from crates around Goblin Village (or off goblin drops), make blue dye (woad leaves from Wyson the gardener + Aggie in Draynor) and orange dye (combine red+yellow dye from Aggie), dye one mail orange and one blue, leave the third plain. Show the generals orange (rejected), then blue (rejected) -- they settle on the original plain brown. This is the real gate on the RFD 'Freeing the Goblin generals' subquest.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Second Mahjarrat-series quest, novice, with a good/evil branch (same structure as Temple of Ikov). Ceril Carnillean says cultists stole his family armour; head to the cave south-east of the Ardougne clock tower and talk to Clivet to learn about the Mahjarrat Hazeel. Choose a side: siding with Ceril means refusing Clivet, turning 5 sewer valves in specific directions to reach the hideout raft, killing Alomone (level 13) for Carnillean armour, then reporting to Ceril for a fake ending -- search the upstairs cupboard for Hazeel's mark and poison and show Ceril to fire the butler for the real reward. Siding with Hazeel means accepting Clivet's offer, poisoning Ceril's cooking range (accidentally kills the dog Scruffy instead), getting Hazeel's mark from Clivet, turning the same 5 valves, then helping Butler Jones steal a restoration scroll from a locked chest in Ceril's house and giving it to Alomone to begin the resurrection. Choice also has a minor effect on Secrets of the North.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Short experienced-tier miniquest. The Strange Old Man asks you to retrieve a strange icon guarded by the six Barrows Brothers — defeat all six, then loot the icon from the Barrows reward chest (keep at least 6 free inventory slots or you must redo all six brothers to get it), and return it to him to finish. Wiki lists 'Required for completing: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire' (Twisted Tale/lore cross-reference, not a normal prerequisite chain). Promoted into this batch from a stalled wave3:morytania-prayer-pvm queue entry per DEPTH_BACKLOG Band C guidance.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Must own a cat and beat a level-91 Possessed Priest plus one randomly-assigned level 75-81 elemental guardian (Apmeken/Crondis/Scabaras magic, or Het melee - determined by which canopic jar you're given). Give the Wanderer a tinderbox+waterskin(4), enter the pyramid (no teleporting out once inside - aggressive lvl-84 mummies + poison scarabs, use minimap-click nav to dodge traps, jump the pit at 20%+ run energy). Solve a golden-bird door puzzle to trigger flashback 1. Drop your cat to answer the Sphinx's riddle (answer: 9) or lose the cat, then see the High Priest. Flashback 2: re-enter pyramid, take the correct canopic jar from the west room, kill the guardian that spawns, take the jar out (crossing the pit twice), then return and drop the jar back before leaving. Prep for the ceremony: give the Embalmer linen+salt+sap, give the Carpenter willow logs for a holy symbol. Flashback 3: use the holy symbol on a sarcophagus in the east room, kill the level-91 Possessed Priest, talk High Priest. Flashback 4: talk High Priest again in the temple to finish. Post-quest: equipping the catspeak amulet and asking your cat about "being confused" unlocks bonus lore cutscenes. High spine value: opens Sophanem/Menaphos region access.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements. Collect the 4 coloured beads (buy on GE or kill imps -- F2P spots near Falador's south entrance or Karamja volcano top; members near Ardougne Monastery/Chasm of Fire/Charcoal furnace), then hand them to Wizard Mizgog atop the Wizards' Tower for the amulet of accuracy.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements. A whodunit inside a manor reached by boat south of Lumbridge Swamp: empty a rainwater barrel for the manor key, then solve three room puzzles for coloured gem keys (slash a painting for the ruby key, play D-E-A-D on a piano for the emerald key, enter a gem sequence Sapphire-Diamond-Zenyte-Emerald-Onyx-Ruby in a fireplace for the sapphire key). Use all three keys on the final blue door, dodge the hidden killer's knife throws by aiming a mirror at their wardrobe, then fight and unmask Abigale as the killer.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Short novice fetch quest: run past thieves (two level-14, one level-26) in a stone-circle den west of the monastery to recover a stolen child's blanket, then help Brother Cedric prep a birthday party by giving him a jug of water and some logs/a plank before returning to Brother Omad.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements (Kandarin-region quest, included in this bundle's spine). Old man Sinclair is dead; find flypaper and a dagger inside the mansion, use flour+flypaper on silver items from two thread-colour-matched suspects' bedrooms to dust prints and match one to the dagger. Ask Gossip outside and the Seers' Village pub poison salesman who bought poison, confront the matched suspect about their motive, then search the item they claim to have used the poison on to confirm before reporting to a guard.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Talk to Orlando Smith to start, then click the plaque in front of each of 14 creature displays (North room: Lizard/Battle tortoise/Dragon/Wyvern; East room: Snail/Snake/Sea slug/Monkey; South room: Kalphite Queen/Terrorbird; West room: Penguin/Mole/Camel/Leech) and answer 3 multiple-choice questions correctly per display -- answers come from that display's own Natural historian's lecture or the wiki page text itself, and a good approach is simply guessing since only 3-of-several need to be right. Once all 3 for a display are answered correctly its questions are replaced by the creature's common+scientific name. Return to Orlando after all 14 displays are done for the xp reward.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Edmond's daughter Elena vanished into plague-locked West Ardougne. His wife Alrena makes a gas mask from dwellberries; soften the mud behind the house with 4 buckets of water then dig under the wall with the spade, use rope on the blocked pipe grill to get through. Inside, show Jethick Elena's picture, learn from the Rehnison family she was taken to the barred plague house. Cure Bravek's hangover (mix chocolate dust + bucket of milk + snape grass) to get a warrant past the Mourners guarding that house, find and free Elena, then return to Edmond via the manhole. One of the Ardougne-diary prereq quests alongside Sheep Shearer and Clock Tower.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Sneak a gas mask (via pigeon + bird feed distraction) past the mourner watchtower into West Ardougne to retrieve Elena's distillator, fetch touch paper from the Rimmington chemist, hand plague-sample vials to 3 of the chemist's henchmen around a campfire and collect the processed samples back, then smuggle the sample and paper through the guarded Varrock fence disguised in a priest gown to deliver them to Guidor, and finally report to King Lathas in Ardougne Castle.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.instruction: Complete Rag and Bone Man I
steps.detail: No requirements. Kill 8 low-level creatures for their bones (giant rat, unicorn, bear, ram, goblin, big frog, monkey, giant bat -- all reachable on a walk from Varrock through Lumbridge Swamp to Karamja), buy vinegar from Fortunato in Draynor to soak them, then boil each in a pot over a fire at the Odd Old Man's hut. Buying 27 extra vinegar jugs now (35 total) saves a trip if planning Rag and Bone Man II next; direct prerequisite for that sequel quest.

refs.title: Rag and Bone Man I/Quick guide

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements. Fred wants 20 balls of wool. Fastest route is simply buying 20 balls of wool (tradeable) and handing them in; the 'intended' route -- shear sheep in the pen with shears, spin the wool at a spinning wheel -- is optional and grants +50 bonus Crafting xp over just buying. Same opener tier as Cook's Assistant, designed to walk new players through Lumbridge.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Requires beating level-69 Draugen and an unarmed/unarmoured fight vs Koschei the deathless (first 3 forms; 4th form is safe to "die" to, even on HCIM). Order: lose a drinking contest to Manni, get Hunters' Talisman from Sigli, talk Olaf, obtain a lyre (craft from swaying-tree branch via Lalli's stew quest-within-quest, or farm Lanzig's 1/17 drop). Spin golden wool in Seers' Village if crafting the lyre, buy low-alcohol keg from Poison Salesman. Back in Rellekka: use beer on council workman for strange object, enchant lyre with raw fish at a strange altar near the bridge, hunt the Draugen via talisman, light strange object and use on longhall pipe, play enchanted lyre on stage, then win the rematch drinking contest vs Manni with the low-alcohol keg swap. Sigmund/Swensen trial is a long NPC-chase-and-recall-order chain culminating in paying Askeladden 5,000 coins then repeating the NPC list in reverse order. Thorvald trial: bank everything (no weapons/armour/runes allowed), craft a dramen/celastrus staff underground, fight Koschei's first 3 forms unarmed. Peer the Seer trial: bank all bankable items, solve a riddle (each line gives one letter of a 4-letter word) then a multi-step item-transformation puzzle (bucket/jug/vase/disks/red herring) to unlock a trapdoor exit. Finish by reporting to Brundt. High spine value: gates Fremennik Isles (Neitiznot/Jatizso/Miscellania/Etceteria) sea access.

hints.value: agility +2812, attack +2812, crafting +2812, defence +2812, fishing +2812, fletching +2812, hitpoints +2812, strength +2812, thieving +2812, woodcutting +2812
hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Novice quest; only real gate is being able to kill or flee a level 13 skeleton. Talk to Father Aereck about the haunted Lumbridge graveyard, head to Father Urhney's hut west of Lumbridge Swamp and talk to him, then go to the small room in the graveyard, equip the Ghostspeak amulet and talk to the coffin-ghost. Go to the Wizards' Tower basement and search the altar for a ghost's skull (a level 13 skeleton spawns but does not need to be killed, just leave), then use the skull on the open coffin to finish. Required for: Animal Magnetism, Curse of the Empty Lord, Ghosts Ahoy, Making History, Nature Spirit, Creature of Fenkenstrain.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Research 3 independent historical leads for the outpost's curator Jorral, in any order: dig up a buried chest near Castle Wars with a silver merchant's key, trade a ghost in Port Phasmatys a sapphire amulet for a scroll, and pass a lengthy trivia quiz with the warrior Dron in Rellekka. Report all 3 findings back to Jorral, then relay them to King Lathas.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 112 Khazard warlord (safespottable). Talk to King Bolren (teleports you to the north-west corner of the maze), give Commander Montai 6 logs on the battlefield's south-west edge, then agree to help further. Talk to all 3 tracker gnomes around the Khazard Stronghold to get 4-digit ballista coordinates from riddles, then fire the south-west ballista with that code. Climb into the stronghold over the crumbled wall, ignore or kill the level 48 Khazard commanders, take an orb of protection from the upstairs chest, exit, and report to Elkoy to be sent to the village centre. Track down and kill the level 112 Khazard warlord north-west of the battlefield (safespottable near the West Ardougne wall) for the other two orbs, then return all 3 orbs to King Bolren to finish. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Path of Glouphrie, Medium Western Provinces Diary, Medium Varrock Diary.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Very short early quest, beats level-34 Count Draynor (safespottable, high healing - garlic weakens him and is strongly recommended). Talk Morgan, grab garlic from his upstairs cupboard. Travel to the Blue Moon Inn in Varrock, buy a beer, talk Dr Harlow twice for a stake, buy a hammer from the general store just north of the inn. Head to Draynor Manor basement (east stairs, not the west room), open the coffin and kill Count Draynor with combat gear (hammer/stake just need to be in inventory, not actively used). Free F2P quest, low reqs, no known deeper dependency chain.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Intermediate quest requiring the ability to withstand/evade level 86 Fire Giants and a level 84 Moss Guardian with no equipment (Glarial's Tomb bans gear). Talk to Almera, raft to the island, talk to Hudon then Hadley, find a Book on Baxtorian in an upstairs bookcase and read it. Enter the Tree Gnome Village maze/dungeon (worth starting Tree Gnome Village here too), find a key in a crate, unlock the west gate, and get Glarial's pebble from Golrie (keep it banked for Roving Elves later). Bank everything except the pebble, food, and a teleport method, use the pebble on Glarial's Tombstone east of the Waterfall to enter, and retrieve Glarial's amulet and urn from the tomb. Return with rope + 6 air/water/earth runes + amulet + urn: cross the raft/rope route into the waterfall dungeon, unlock the door past the Fire Giants, place one of each rune on 3 pillars, wear the amulet at Glarial's statue to raise the ground, then pour the urn into the chalice to finish. Required for: Desert Treasure I, Legends' Quest, Medium Kandarin Diary, Roving Elves, The Path of Glouphrie.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: A boy's ball landed in a scary witch's garden. Sneak into her house (key under the potted plant by the front door), grab gloves and a magnet from the basement, then trap a mouse with cheese and the magnet. Cross the garden to the fountain and shed without the Witch seeing you (stick to hedges, never walk on grass, or you must repeat the break-in); getting caught mid-fight also drops your combat stats if you grab the ball too early. Open the shed and fight the Witch's experiment through all four escalating forms (levels 19/30/42/53, corners of the shed are safespottable), take the ball, then sneak/teleport back out and return it to the boy.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Hetty the witch wants help brewing a potion. Gather four ingredients: kill a rat in the archery shop west of Hetty for a rat's tail, buy an eye of newt from Betty (3gp), kill a giant rat at the chapel south of Port Sarim for raw rat meat and burn it on Hetty's fireplace, and pick an onion from the field north of Rimmington. Bring all four to Hetty and drink from her cauldron to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No skill or quest requirements at all -- can be done immediately. Get a plague jacket/trousers and cattleprod from Doctor Orbon, then prod one sheep of each colour (blue, yellow, green, red -- each in its own quadrant of the pen north-west of the north Ardougne bank) through the gate into the enclosure, feed them poisoned sheep feed until they die, then burn the corpses on the incinerator and report back. No skill xp reward, just 3,100 coins, but unusually generous at 4 quest points for a Novice-difficulty quest.

steps.instruction: Complete A Tail of Two Cats
steps.detail: Sequel to Gertrude's Cat: Bob the cat has gone missing. Get Hild to enchant a catspeak amulet with 5 death runes, then use it to Locate Bob repeatedly across a list of world-hoppable spawn spots (western Varrock anvil and Draynor Manor garden are the fastest camp spots). Visit Gertrude and Reldo in Varrock Palace library to learn Bob's lineage, then travel to Sophanem to consult the Sphinx (desert-region travel, needs desert clothing or waterskins). Do a set of fetch-quest chores for Unferth at his house (grow potatoes, make the bed, light the fireplace, feed him cake+milk, cut his hair with shears) before a final disguise-and-cutscene sequence with the Apothecary in Varrock to cure Bob's "sickness" (white doctor/nurse outfit required).

refs.title: A Tail of Two Cats/Quick guide

steps.detail: Simple pub-crawl miniquest: talk your way past a Barbarian Guard into the Outpost, then visit 10 named inns/bars across Gielinor in any order (Blue Moon Inn Varrock, Jolly Boar Inn, Rising Sun Inn Falador, Rusty Anchor Inn Port Sarim, Karamja Spirits Bar Musa Point, Dead Man's Chest Brimhaven, Flying Horse Inn Ardougne, Forester's Arms Seers' Village, Blurberry Bar Tree Gnome Stronghold, Dragon Inn Yanille), picking the barcrawl dialogue option at each. Return to the same Barbarian Guard at the Outpost gate to finish. No combat, no items needed beyond travel means.

Archaeologist Willow needs her old crew reunited to break into sealed ruins west of Ice Mountain. Recruit Checkal in Barbarian Village: train with Atlas in The Long Hall tavern, then use the Flex emote on him with cooked meat/beer from the tavern tables in hand. Recruit Marley at the yew trees in Edgeville (must speak to him first), then get the steak sandwich recipe from the cook at Varrock's Blue Moon Inn and craft one (knife on cooked meat with bread in inventory) to bring back to Marley. Recruit Burntof at Falador's Rising Sun Inn by buying him a beer and winning/playing a round of rock-paper-scissors. With all three recruited, meet Willow at the Ruins of Camdozaal entrance west of Ice Mountain; after the entry cutscene, defeat the level-25 Ancient Guardian (fight it directly, or mine its 4 corner structural pillars instead of combat) to finish.

Requires 12 quest points and the ability to dodge/evade level 33 Black Knights (no kills required). Disguised in an iron chainbody + bronze med helm, infiltrate the Black Knights' Fortress, push a hidden wall and navigate ladders to eavesdrop at a grill, then bluff past a guard's chat to reach the dining hall, push another hidden wall, and jam the secret weapon's works with a cabbage (do not eat it) before reporting back to Sir Amik Varze.

First quest in the Temple Knight series. After Sir Amik sends you to Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador Park, you must clear 5 of 7 possible randomized puzzle rooms in a row (order and selection random per playthrough) -- always including a Sir Leye fight (use the warhammer or fight unarmed; any bladed weapon on the killing blow fails the room). Other rooms are logic/memory puzzles (river-crossing animals, missing-statue memory test, multi-step chemistry door puzzle, riddle-based combination lock, multiple-choice riddles, an 8-second stand-still test). Failing any room sends you back to Falador to restart the 5-room gauntlet from scratch. Short quest but gates Wanted! and the Medium Falador Diary, so it's a real progression choke point despite its low difficulty rating.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Varlamore's opening quest. A Varlamore delegation has arrived in Varrock; speak to Noah or Alina about it, then covertly tail a suspicious guard through Varrock without being spotted, ducking behind buildings (Lowe's Archery Emporium, the building south of it, Old Man Yarlo's house, near Aubury's Rune Shop) to stay hidden. Report to Sergeant Tobyn at the castle wall, then identify and mark four bandits disguised as guards by their distinctive appearance/weapons/stance around Varrock Square, and report back to the sergeant to finish. No combat or skill requirements — this quest unlocks the rest of the Varlamore quest line.

Murder-mystery on the island of Aldarin. Break into the house north of Patzi via the south window (wait for the guard to move) to steal a servant uniform, then wear it to enter Villa Lucens through the Head Butler (who holds your gear). Talk to the four nobles (Constantinius, Cozyac, Pavo, Xocotla) and Patzi, then search the wine cellar for clues (antique wine, injured man, jugs, box, stool, pottery, Livius' body, wine storage). Talk to guards Stradius/Hutza, pickpocket all six suspects, inspect the physical evidence (wine labels, threatening note, shipping contract, drinking flask), then accuse and fight Adala (level 49) -- she's innocent. Climb the Loose rocks shortcut, go backstage, question the Costumer, search the poisoned crate/secret bookshelf passage/stained costume rack, then accuse and fight Naiatli on stage to close the case. Post-quest: claim masks/tray from the Head Butler, needle from the Costumer, pickpocket Constantinius for a chest key (opens the cellar's fancy chest), search the SW fountain for an icon, and use it on the NE Aldarin statue for a bonus reward.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Varlamore/Civitas illa Fortis quest. Talk to Emelio then the Spice Merchant in the Bazaar about a missing delivery (open the mystery box with code 2546). Visit Alba north of Ortus Farm, then fight the level-113 Dire Wolf Alpha in the wolf den west of the farm (multicombat, melee+ranged simultaneously -- bring food). Return, deliver a specific mix of meat/salad/spice/sauce portions to make a test kebab, then fight the level-193 Minotaur in Fortis Colosseum for Lelia -- watch for "Moo!"/"Moooo!" voice cues to switch Protect from Melee/Magic (the double-moo signals a faster attack). Finish by reporting to Emelio.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Intermediate quest, the proper introduction to Varlamore's capital. Help Prince Itzla investigate a plot against his father Servius by rounding up six Knights of Varlamore scattered around Civitas illa Fortis — a pickpocket hunt in the bazaar, a smuggling-crate search on the docks, a sparring fight against knight Mezan, and reuniting drunk Azali with her partner. A forged letter gets an innocent knight killed by Ennius Tullus; learn of the doomsday cult Twilight Emissaries and their belief in the Final Dawn, then fight off 8 cultists at the Teomat as Servius is mysteriously wounded despite no cultist reaching him. Required for: Perilous Moons, The Heart of Darkness.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No skill/quest gate; the wiki's own 'requirements' line is just 'ability to run past level 53 Ogres' -- not a level check, a survivability note for the red-cog room. Find 4 coloured cogs in the dungeon under the tower and fit each into its matching spindle one at a time (can't carry more than one cog at once): blue cog sits in a rat cell; black cog needs a bucket of water or ice gloves to cool it before picking up; red cog is guarded by ogres; white cog requires poisoning dungeon rats (rat poison + food trough) to unhinge a gate. Reward is a flat 500 coins, no xp. Fills out the Ardougne-diary prereq set alongside Plague City and Sheep Shearer.

No skill/quest requirements; must be able to defeat a level 27 demon (Delrith) aided by dark wizards, using Silverlight in melee. Give Aris 1gp, then collect three keys to Silverlight from three Varrock Palace NPCs: Captain Rovin (talk, NW tower 2F), a rusty key found via bucket-of-water-on-drain then a manhole dungeon crawl, and Wizard Traiborn at the Wizards' Tower in exchange for 25 un-noted bones. Return the keys to Sir Prysin, wield Silverlight, fight through dark wizards at Varrock's south entrance, then banish Delrith with the incantation Aris/Wally gave earlier (randomized per player, re-askable from Aris if forgotten).

Requires only the ability to survive a level 22 skeleton. Explore the spooky Draynor Manor for Veronica's missing fiance Ernest: gather poison and fish food to make poisoned fish food, solve a lever/door puzzle in the basement to fetch an oil can, dig up a key from the compost heap outside, retrieve a pressure gauge from a poisoned fountain, and grab a rubber tube from a skeleton-guarded room, then deliver all the parts to Professor Oddenstein to turn Ernest back from a chicken.

No hard skill/quest gate -- high combat is only recommended. Core loop: kill Forthos Dungeon monsters (Sarachnis 1/5, red dragon 1/10, undead druid 1/20, baby red dragon 1/25, temple spider 1/30) for a drop table of tattered sun/moon/temple pages, need 4 of each to fill 3 tomes found in the dungeon's Temple Library, then hand the filled tomes to Logosia in the Arceuus Library. Designed to piggyback on spider/zombie/red-dragon/Sarachnis Slayer tasks as a passive grind. NOTE: the depth:04 queue payload's parenthetical '(prayer-book unlock)' for this quest is NOT substantiated anywhere in the quest's own page (Details/Walkthrough/Reward/Trivia) -- no prayer-book, spellbook or altar unlock is mentioned; the only reward is the one-time 10k xp lamp. Treated as an unverified/likely-wrong brief claim, not repeated as fact here.

This IS Tutorial Island itself -- no separate /Quick guide exists (search resolves to the identical page/section map for both titles). Walk through 8 instructor buildings in order (Gielinor Guide, Survival Expert, Master Chef, Quest Guide, Mining Instructor, Combat Instructor -- kill 2 level-3 giant rats here, Account Guide, Brother Brace, Magic Instructor) doing character customization first. Ends by choosing Ironman Mode restrictions with the Ironman tutor if applicable, then stepping onto the boat to the mainland. Every new account starts here; it is the universal quest-chain root.

Tiny two-NPC errand: speak to either Fabia (thinks she was to meet Xilo by 'the magic tree') or Xilo (thinks she was to meet Fabia by 'the special tree') -- each is confused because they described the same rendezvous tree differently. Then speak to whichever of the two you did NOT talk to first; she leaves her spot to go join the other, resolving the miscommunication and completing the miniquest. No requirements, no combat, no reward beyond flavour lore per the Miniquests page's own table (Requirements/Length/Unlock all listed None/Short/None).

First Camelot-series quest. Ask King Arthur then Sir Gawain and Sir Lancelot in Camelot for leads, sneak into Keep Le Faye by hiding in crates near the Catherby docks, fight Sir Mordred (lvl 39) on the top floor but do not kill him or interrupt the cutscene when Morgan Le Faye begs for his life -- sparing him gets the recipe for freeing Merlin. Gather bat bones (kill giant bats), a black candle (bucket of wax + candle maker in Catherby), and Excalibur (pass Lady of the Lake's bread-to-a-beggar test at the Port Sarim jeweller's). Read the chaos altar inscription in Varrock's Zamorakian chapel for the magic words. Back at Camelot, light the candle and drop (not bury) the bat bones on the garden symbol to summon Thrantax the Mighty (level 92, do not get the incantation wrong or he attacks), grant your boon to free Merlin, then use Excalibur on the crystal in the south-east tower. Gates into Heroes' Quest and Holy Grail.

The Sailing-skill unlock quest. Accept a shipwreck-salvage job from Will/Anne, helm a ship south through the Bay of Sarim to east of the Karamja Shipyard (trim sails on wind gusts for speed), deploy the salvaging hook. At the Pandemonium Pub talk to 'Squawking' Steve Beanie and Ribs about the map, then get a raft from Junior Jim in exchange for a salvaged cup; build a cargo hold on the raft with the hammer+saw. Final leg: sail the raft north through Mudskipper Sound/Bay of Sarim to Port Sarim, load cargo from the ledger table (must have empty hands, no weapons), sail back and deliver it. Talk to Steve Beanie to finish.

Classic F2P-adjacent quest, needs a level-4 Gardener defeated or evaded. Sail to Karamja (30 coins), buy Karamjan rum, gather 10 bananas, and get a job with Luthas the banana farmer smuggling rum in a banana crate (fill the OUTSIDE crate, not the bedroom one). Return to Port Sarim, get a white apron from Gerrant's Fishy Business, work for Wydin to retrieve the rum from the crate, and trade it to Redbeard Frank for a key. Use the key on a chest upstairs in Varrock's Blue Moon Inn to read a pirate message pointing to Falador Park. Dig there (kill/evade the Gardener) to unearth the treasure casket.

Prince Ali has been kidnapped by Lady Keli; Hassan hires you to break him out. Osman sends you to Ned in Draynor Village for a disguise wig (3 balls of wool) and to Aggie for skin paste (ashes+flour+water+redberries) and yellow dye, then combine into a disguise. Get a key impression from Lady Keli at the jailhouse east of Draynor and smelt a matching bronze key with a bronze bar at any furnace. Talk your way past the level-26 jail guards with 3 beers for Joe, use the rope on Lady Keli, unlock the cell with the smelted key, and hand the wig/paste/pink skirt to Prince Ali so he can disguise himself out. Report back to Hassan to finish.

Sequel to Icthlarin's Little Helper: sneak into Menaphos through the Sophanem dungeon labyrinth. Talk to the High Priest, then Jex in Sophanem's NE building, descend and follow the tile-marker path (green=direction, yellow=step carefully, red=warning) through floor/wall traps, crusher blocks, sand pits and scarab pit-traps to a ladder -- keep Protect from Magic up and auto-retaliate off; one scarab trap drops you a level into multi-combat. Search Kaleef's corpse, read the parchment, then shuttle between Maisa (west) and Osman (Al Kharid, then Sophanem's gates) to arrange a second, secret entrance. Re-enter via that route and kill the level-191 Giant Scarab boss (safespottable near Kaleef's body with Protect from Missiles if maging/ranging, but it can spawn mages/riders and extinguishes bullseye lanterns -- bring a tinderbox). Talk to Osman, optionally grab Keris for the Desert hard diary (don't teleport out if going for that task; return and kill a locust rider with it), then return to the High Priest to finish. Gates Beneath Cursed Sands and continues the desert quest series toward Desert Treasure II.

steps.detail: No requirements. Classic go-between quest: relay a letter from Juliet (upper balcony of the mansion west of Varrock's west bank) to Romeo in the square, then fetch cadava berries (grow west of the SE Varrock mine) for the Apothecary near the west bank to brew a sleeping potion. Give the potion to Juliet and tell Romeo to meet her in the crypt to finish. Prerequisite quest feeding into Defender of Varrock's requirement chain.

No requirements or items needed -- easiest of the orphan gates. Duke Horacio gives an air talisman; take it to Archmage Sedridor in the Wizards' Tower basement, who sends it on to Aubury in his Varrock rune shop as a research package; Aubury swaps it for notes to carry back to Sedridor, who then explains Runecraft basics and returns the talisman. The single most-required prereq quest on the wiki: opens rune essence mining/Runecraft entirely, and directly gates Aubury's essence-mine teleport (Varrock Diary Easy task) and Sedridor's essence-mine teleport (Lumbridge & Draynor Diary Easy task).

Unequip any Saradomin/Guthix items before talking to the Mage of Zamorak or he refuses to speak (inventory is fine, no need to bank). Core task: he hands over a scrying orb, then you travel to the rune essence mine from 3 of 5 possible start points (Aubury/Varrock, Wizards' Tower basement, East Ardougne, Brimstail's cave, or Wizards' Guild) so the orb records readings, then return to Varrock to finish. This is the gap-filler the alch-fodder/rune-supply chain needs: it opens the Abyss as a fast essence-mining + teleport hub feeding into Enter the Abyss's own required-for list (Devious Minds, Temple of the Eye, partial Wanted!, Wilderness Easy Diary).

steps.detail: One-off restoration errand, not on the quest list. Olbertus's burner/shrine sits under a curse; offer eligible draconic bones (babydragon, dragon, lava dragon, superior dragon) to raise its 'faith level' from 0 toward 200 points (giving 5 unnoted dragonstones up front instead jumps you straight to the 100-point mark). Faith thresholds give flavour messages at 0/50/100/150/200; at 100 the burner starts granting +300% Prayer xp for offered bones/ashes; at 200 a temple key appears, permanently unlocking the dungeon's remaining gates/shortcut, though the xp boost itself has no further threshold beyond 100. Use the 'Check-Faith' option on the burner to gauge progress at any time.

Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about quests, check the bright blue book on the top shelf of the bookcase, read it, then talk to Reldo again to start. You must then pick a side, Phoenix Gang or Black Arm Gang, and need a partner going the opposite gang to finish (official chat-channel 'OSRS SOA' helps pair players). Gang-specific inner steps ?? (walkthrough branches into two separate subpages, not fetched at quick-guide grain). Once your half of the shield is retrieved, take it to Curator Haig Halen in Varrock Museum, trade certificate halves with your partner (ironman: use halves on partner), combine the two halves, then hand the completed certificate to King Roald for the reward.

Unnamed-in-game miniquest (config name spiritofadventure_*), released 23 July 2025 with Varlamore: The Final Dawn. Wearing a ghostspeak amulet, talk to ghost Bernina, who died in the wreck that named Shipwreck Cove and lost her diamond necklace to a salvager. Find salvager Jasper at Salvager Overlook (he sold it on due to bad luck) to learn it went to a mountain climber. Grab logs+tinderbox from houses south of Jasper, climb the Proudspire to find climber Bernard, light a fire for him (with the logs/tinderbox) to learn he's carrying the necklace up the mountain to honour his late mother -- who turns out to be Bernina. Return to Bernina to relay this; she teleports to join Bernard atop the Proudspire. Return to Bernard so you can ghost-interpret for the reunion, then climb to the peak obelisk and speak to Bernard there to finish. No combat, no item/xp reward.

Talk to Captain Tock at the crossroads north of Port Sarim, agree to help, then meet him again on the single dock west of Rimmington to sail to Corsair Cove. On arrival speak to Ithoi the Navigator first (west along the beach, upstairs) or the other NPC dialogues break. Talk to Arsen the Thief and Cabin Boy Colin (same upstairs room, first house west of docks) and Gnocci the Cook (upstairs NW building, grab a tinderbox and a spade nearby if needed) about their 'curses'. Get the ogre artefact from Captain Tock, return it to Chief Tess in the Corsair Cove Dungeon. Dig a sand lump near the tropical tree by the fishing spot, use the telescope on Ithoi's hut deck, then report findings back to Gnocci, Arsen, and Colin. Question the same four NPCs again about who's the real traitor, culminating in confronting Ithoi twice, torching driftwood under his hut with your tinderbox to catch him not-sick, and reporting to Captain Tock. Enter the instanced fight and kill Ithoi the Navigator (level 35, protect from magic negates his damage), then report the kill to Captain Tock to finish. Recommended 20+ Combat plus weapon/armour/food for the fight.

Hot-and-cold treasure hunt across ~11 fixed sites Gielinor-wide (map pins include areas near Rellekka, Ardougne, Tree Gnome Stronghold, Falador, Varrock, Mudskipper Point, Lumbridge Swamp, Al Kharid). Use 'Feel Enchanted key' to get a two-part temperature reading: an absolute distance band (freezing/very cold/cold/warm/hot/very hot/burning hot/steaming, based on Chebyshev distance ignoring obstacles) plus a relative warmer/colder/same-as-last-reading hint; walk in a direction, compare readings, and triangulate N/S then E/W until 'the key is steaming' (within 4 squares), then dig with a spade. 3 of the sites hold a unique god mjolnir/spear (Saradomin/Guthix/Zamorak) obtainable no other way besides trading; the rest give a spread of ores/runes/ammo. Not gated by combat.

Talk to Cassius by the Lumbridge pond to start. Talk to Gillie Groats then Seth Groats about their productive cows, search the shelves in Seth's farmhouse for 'The Groats principles' book, and bring it to Cassius for a milk sample. Drink the sample in front of Cassius to get a second sample for Duke Horacio; deliver it, then talk to Gillie Groats and drink the sample in front of her for a permit to deal with the bull. Open the pen in the NE corner of the cow farm and fight the bull (recommended 15+ Combat), then report back to Gillie Groats and Cassius, and finally talk to Gillie Groats again to collect the rewards.

No requirements, tutorial-tier treasure hunt intro quest. Talk to Veos in the Sheared Ram pub, Lumbridge, to start. Dig at 4 marked spots in sequence: by Bob's Brilliant Axes (west of the plant against the wall), behind Lumbridge Castle near the kitchen door, north-west of the Draynor Village jail by the wheat farm, and in the pig pen next to Martin the Master Gardener. Use the resulting ancient casket on Veos at the Port Sarim docks to finish.

Talk to Veos (found at the northernmost dock of Port Sarim, south of the pub) to travel to Port Piscarilius and start; he gives an enchanted scroll, use a feather on it for an enchanted quill. Visit each of the 5 Great Kourend districts and speak with a named store owner about their district (e.g. Jennifer in Shayzien, Munty in Lovakengj). Return to Veos, then travel near the Dark Altar (Games necklace to Wintertodt Camp then run, or fairy ring code CIS) and activate the mysterious orb. Return to Veos to finish.

Short intermediate quest: the Doors of Dinh, sealing away the Wintertodt, are failing. Retrieve Dinh's hammer from the Forsaken Tower to repair them by solving four puzzles — a water-jug coolant puzzle to safely light a furnace without taking damage, a power-grid alignment puzzle, a potion-riddle to cleanse a refinery without poisoning yourself, and a ring/disk-stacking pylon puzzle — then return the hammer to blacksmith Undor. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary.

steps.detail: Gather 5 jungle herbs around Tai Bwo Wannai/Karamja for Trufitus's potion, showing him each new herb before hunting the next: snake weed (marshy vines SW of the village), ardrigal (palm trees NE, near Harpie Bug Swarms), sito foil (scorched earth south of the village), volencia moss (mine rocks SE) and rogue's purse (fungus on cave walls NW, must be searched from the wall, not looted from a Jogre kill).

steps.detail: Speak to the Lumbridge Cook to begin Recipe for Disaster. Unlock the 10-person banquet table.

steps.detail: Novice quest gating a shortcut through White Wolf Mountain. Talk to either dwarf guarding the mountain's tunnel entrances (starting on the west side is faster on foot). Grab garlic from a house south-west of the Seers' Village bank if needed, take a spade to McGrubor's Wood and dig up 3 red vine worms from the vine just west of fairy ring ALS. Head to Hemenster, buy a fishing rod from Grandpa Jack if you don't have one, enter with the fishing pass, put garlic in the north pipes (finish the dialogue), pay Bonzo 5 coins and start fishing once the Sinister Stranger clears the pipes. After winning the contest, bring the trophy back to the dwarf to finish. Required for: Recipe for Disaster (Freeing the Mountain Dwarf), Forgettable Tale..., Between a Rock..., Land of the Goblins.

steps.detail: Talk to the Squire in the White Knights' Castle courtyard about his lost ceremonial sword. Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about the Imcando dwarves (skip if Thurgo was already met via The Giant Dwarf). Find Thurgo south of Port Sarim near the anvil (north of fairy ring AIQ) and give him a redberry pie, talk to him again, then return to the Squire. Climb to the White Knights' Castle 1st floor, go west and up the east staircase, and search the cupboard in Sir Vyvin's bedroom for a portrait while he isn't looking. Bring the portrait (and iron bars) to Thurgo. Enter the Asgarnian Ice Dungeon via the trapdoor east of Thurgo, follow the path west/north/east, and mine blurite ore along the southern wall of the icy area (mine a second if doing the Falador easy diary). Give Thurgo the iron bars and blurite ore to forge the replacement sword, then deliver the sword to the Squire to finish.

steps.detail: Talk to an examiner to attempt (and auto-fail) a first exam, then get a sealed letter from Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum. North of the Exam Centre near the blue urn hilltop, search bushes for a teddy bear, take a panning tray, use a cup of tea on the panning guide, pan for a Special cup (and an opal if needed), and pickpocket the nearby workman for an animal skull and specimen brush. Deliver the teddy/cup/skull to the three named students around the dig (NW, E, S respectively, talk twice each). Retake all 3 exams at the Exam Centre picking the correct dialogue options (fail is not possible once you know the answers) to get Level 1/2/3 certificates, re-visiting the students between exams (the female student demands an opal for the 2nd). Save the certificates for the museum curator. Back at the Exam Centre's western room, get a rock pick and specimen jar from cupboards, use the trowel on soil in a level-3 dig site until you find an ancient talisman (can take a while), then talk to Terry Balando and use his invitation on any digsite workman except the one by the western winch. Rope down the western winch to grab arcenia root and search bricks, then rope down the northern winch to talk to Doug Deeping about the rocks. South of the site, unlock a chest for chemical powder, get an unidentified liquid from a barrel (careful — it deals 25 damage and breaks the vial if dropped), grind charcoal, then give the powder/liquid to Terry Balando and mix nitroglycerin+ammonium nitrate, then charcoal, then arcenia root into a chemical compound. Use the compound then a tinderbox on the bricks down the western winch, enter the chamber (watch for level-22 skeletons), grab the stone tablet, and hand it to Terry Balando to finish.

steps.detail: Third Sailing-skill quest. Arhein wants to test whether a duck can track ocean currents but is blocked by Catherby bureaucracy. Fill in form cr-4p for Councillor Catherine using charcoal (answers are arbitrary but must be repeated later -- picking the first option every time is simplest). She reveals the by-law needs mayoral approval, and the current mayor died -- Catherby has had 25 consecutive fish mayors. Buy a mayoral fishbowl and tiny net from Harry (50 coins) and fish a new Mayor of Catherby from an aquarium, then get it a mayoral chain from Arhein. Redo the form quiz for Catherine (only wrong answers need repeating), sign form 7r4-5h with the new mayor, and return it to change the by-law. Take a boat out east near the Obelisk of Water, find a ripple, and use the current duck on it to track the current to shore near Holgart, then return the duck to Arhein to finish. Needs a boat docked at Catherby for the final leg.

steps.detail: Talk to Irena south of Shantay Pass to start. At the Desert Mining Camp entrance, goad the Mercenary Captain (level 47, safespottable/cannonable) into attacking via dialogue and kill him for the gate key — do not skip the dialogue or enter without full waterskins, both can get you jailed or killed by heat damage. Unequip all weapons/armour before entering the gate (graceful is fine if not worn over the required desert clothing) — equipping gear or removing the disguise inside gets you jailed. Trade with the male slave on the east wall for slave clothes when no guard is watching (getting caught = jail; escape by bending the window bars, climbing out over the wall/cliff, and re-entering the gate), equip the clothes, and enter the mine north. Follow the path to a guarded cave entrance and talk to a guard until he mentions a Tenti pineapple, then leave the mine, fully unequip the slave robes, and exit camp via the front gate (or fairy ring BIQ) — leaving in disguise through either the gate or the mine door also risks jail. At Bedabin Camp (west of the mining camp) talk to Al Shabim in the south tent for a Bedabin key. Back in the mining camp (slave outfit on again), climb to the jail building's upper floor, search the southernmost west-wall bookcase for a sailing-books clue, then talk to Captain Siad in the same room and open a chest for technical plans. Show the plans to Al Shabim, then use the northern tent's anvil to smith a bronze dart tip from a bar and fletch it with 10 feathers into a prototype dart; trade it to Al Shabim for the Tenti pineapple. Re-suit in slave clothes, give the pineapple to a mine guard, head east past a mine cart, search barrels for a spare barrel, then search the mine cart to trigger a cutscene ride (redo if it doesn't trigger). Do not talk to Ana — instead follow the west-north-west path and use the spare barrel on her (don't try to teleport with her in your inventory, it drops the barrel). Ride the cart back, put Ana in it then get in yourself, search barrels to find her again, place her on the north-west winch bucket, decline the guard's suspicion-raising offer of help, then surface, operate the winch (south-west of camp), search the barrel west of the winch for Ana, load her onto the wooden cart at camp centre, and talk your way past the mine cart driver. Board the cart and return to Irena for the reward. Downstream note: required for starting Desert Treasure I.

Talk to Sigmund on Lumbridge Castle's 1st floor to start. Ask around (Duke Horacio, the Cook, Hans, Father Aereck, Bob, Gee/Donie) about the collapsed cellar wall until someone mentions a goblin sighting, then report back to the Duke. Climb down the kitchen trapdoor, mine through rubble in the basement, light your light source, and grab a brooch about 10 steps into the cave; show it to the Duke. In Varrock Palace library, talk to Reldo and search the northern bookcase on the west wall for a goblin symbol book; read it. At Goblin Village, talk to the generals and mediate between them until you learn the Goblin Bow and Goblin Salute emotes. Return to the Lumbridge cellar caves and follow the mapped path (clicking the minimap auto-paths it) to reach Mistag; perform the Goblin Bow for him, then let him guide you back to Lumbridge. Report to the Duke (he won't believe you), then pickpocket Sigmund and unlock his chest in the other room for HAM robes (keep for Death to the Dorgeshuun). Head to the HAM lair west of Lumbridge Castle, pick the trapdoor lock, enter, and search a crate south of the ladder for stolen silverware. Return it to the Duke, who fires Sigmund and hands you a signed peace treaty. Go back to the cellar, follow Kazgar down to Mistag, and give him the treaty to trigger the finishing cutscene.

steps.detail: Short intro quest that unlocks the Giants' Foundry minigame; only real requirement is 15 Smithing. Repair 3 broken machines inside the giant-sized workshop (polishing wheel, grindstone, trip hammer) using found parts, then forge one sword start to finish: pick the 3 correctly-shaped mould pieces (forte/blade/tip), pour molten metal from the crucible, cool/heat the preform on the waterfall and lava pool, and alternate trip-hammer -> grindstone -> polishing-wheel passes while watching the heat gauge (working outside the right heat band lowers sword quality).

steps.detail: Newer Varlamore quest; needs Children of the Sun done plus 15 Woodcutting. Comedic frog-politics plot: rig a pond election by distracting the rival yellow frogs (chop the orange tree near them) and sabotaging their lily pad while they're not looking, help nominate a candidate, crack a chest combination ('NALIA') in Marcellus's house, plant a plushy in capybara dung to frame a scapegoat, then kill the 1hp joke-boss Cuthbert (killable via recoil damage on a pure non-combat account) before the final vote. Reward is a new hardwood Farming patch, not the usual quest-cape xp haul.

steps.detail: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 113 Troll General (safespottable). Talk to Denulth south of the Burthorpe teleport, follow the path to the stronghold buying climbing boots from Tenzing along the way, then fight Dad in the arena (safespottable from the gate) -- the fight ends automatically before he dies. Exit north, get past Thrower trolls (sprint, protect from missiles, or agility shortcuts), and enter the stronghold. Inside: kill a Troll General for a prison key (safespottable), pickpocket (30+ Thieving) or kill sleeping Twig and Berry to free both prisoners, escape via games necklace or the secret exit, and talk to Dunstan to finish. Required for: Desert Treasure I, Devious Minds, Eadgar's Ruse, Monkey Madness II, Troll Romance; 15 Agility plus defeating Dad also gates God Wars Dungeon entry (without Ghommal's hilt).

Short intermediate quest: Lord Kandur's son Artur Hosidius has gone missing. Interview the household (Chef Olivia reveals a secret romance and a library research lead), find a book in the Arceuus Library pointing to the sunken Accord of Twill, brave the Crabclaw Caves' stepping-stone crossing to find Artur trapped by a level 36 Sand Snake (weak to magic), kill it, and return the recovered Royal Accord of Twill to Lord Kandur. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Find the chieftain's missing daughter for the isolated Mountain Camp tribe: pole-vault and mud-climb a wilderness route to a talking magic pool, broker peace between the camp and Rellekka (mine a sample of ancient rock, get safe passage), find a hardy food plant on White Wolf Mountain, then kill The Kendal (level 70, melee-only, safespottable) in a cave to recover the daughter's remains and give her a proper burial.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Short desert quest. Read a letter near the broken golem, repair it with 4 soft clay, ask about the portal. Grab a black mushroom (grind for black dye) and a strange implement from the ruins basement (both reusable for Shadow of the Storm if doing that soon after). Talk to Elissa at the Digsite, read Varmen's notes in the Exam Centre bookcase, then talk to Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum about the Uzer statuette, pickpocket him for the display cabinet key, and take the statuette from an upstairs case. Return to Uzer, place the statuette in an empty alcove, rotate 4 statues to all face north to open the portal north; optionally chisel the throne for 2 each of sapphire/emerald/ruby. Make a phoenix quill pen (feather + black dye) and write the golem program on papyrus, then use the strange implement on the golem followed immediately by the program to finish. Region-opener with no known deeper dependency; sets up the desert quest line (Uzer).

steps.detail: Help Dr Fenkenstrain build a monster: read two books for a memorial-opening amulet, dig up a head in the vampyre-infested Haunted Woods, kill an Experiment in the Experiment Cave for body parts, sew the parts together, get a shed key from the Gardener Ghost, extend a garden brush for a conductor mould, smelt a silver bar into a conductor at a furnace, repair the castle's lightning conductor to power the monster, then pickpocket Fenkenstrain for the ring of charos once the monster is animated.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Earn ecto-tokens at the Ectofuntus, then chase down items for a runaway ghost bride's quest: brew nettle tea for an old crone, repair a toy ship and dye its flag to solve a mast puzzle for a hidden treasure map, dig up the Book of Haricanto after fighting a level-32 giant lobster, win a coin-flip game (Rune-Draw) for a translation manual, and collect 10 ghost signatures on a petition before performing a passing-on ceremony for Necrovarus.

steps.detail: Use a wampum belt on Trufitus in Tai Bwo Wannai to properly start the quest, then explore the temple of Ah Za Rhoon for a stone-plaque/scrolls/corpse, the Tomb of Bervirius on Cairn Isle for a locating crystal and bone beads, and finally the Tomb of Rashiliyia where you fight the undead queen Nazastarool across 3 forms; return her corpse to Trufitus's dolmen to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: First Elemental Workshop-series quest. Search a bookcase south-west of the Seers' Village bank for a battered book and read it to start. Slash it with a knife to get a battered key, then use the key on an odd-looking wall in the northern anvil-icon building to enter the underground Elemental Workshop. Turn the east then west water controls green and pull the lever to start the water wheel; fix the bellows with leather+needle (Crafting 20) and pull its lever. Mine an elemental rock (Mining 20) to spawn and kill an earth elemental (level 35) for elemental ore (kill extras now to save time for Elemental Workshop II / a Mind shield later). Fill a stone bowl with lava and use it on the furnace, then smelt the ore with 4 coal into elemental metal (Smithing 20) and smith an elemental shield at the workbench with the slashed book in inventory.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Second Camelot-series quest. King Arthur sends the player after the Holy grail; Merlin points to Entrana and Sir Galahad. Visit Entrana (weapons/armour must be deposited for the monks) and meet a mysterious crone who explains a Fisher King needs help. Get a holy table napkin from Galahad west of McGrubor's Woods, then grab two magic whistles from a room in Draynor Manor. Travel to Brimhaven, head to the guard tower north of the goldmine, and blow a whistle to enter the Fisher King's Realm. Kill the Black Knight Titan (level 120, +1000 magic/ranged defence, flinchable) but the killing blow must be dealt with Excalibur or he heals to full. Ring the grail bell to meet the ailing Fisher King, who asks you to find his son Percival. Get a magic gold feather from King Arthur, follow it to Goblin Village, and find Percival hiding in sacks in the east house; give him a magic whistle. Return to the Fisher Realm (fairy ring code BJR works now) where Percival has become king, then take the Holy grail from upstairs and return it to King Arthur.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Cure a drunk fisherman cursed by mogres. Sober him up by throwing a bucket of water on him, use nettle tea on him (careful not to drink it yourself), talk to him, then brew a hangover cure by combining chocolate dust into a bucket of milk and adding snape grass (again don't drink the intermediate 'chocolatey milk'), and use the finished cure on Skippy to finish. No combat required for the miniquest itself; the unlock (mogres as a valid Slayer task/kill) is used afterward, recommended with 32 Slayer + fishing explosives.

Intermediate quest investigating Port Piscarilius's uniting gang, the Saviours of Kourend. Bribe an informant with cooked stew to learn their hideout in The Warrens, fake-kill a rival gang leader to prove loyalty, then confront the titular Queen of Thieves — revealed to be Lady Shauna Piscarilius, using the gang to target the corrupt rather than terrorise the poor — and burgle a Council member's house for evidence tying him to the Disciples of Yama. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Only requirement is 21 Thieving (boostable); no items needed. Track a totem stolen from the Rantuki tribe through a shipping company's Ardougne depot: relabel a crate to redirect it, get teleported by Wizard Cromperty into collector Lord Handelmort's hideout, crack a spoken door password ('KURT' -- type slowly or it fails), thieve open a locked staircase (needs 21 Thieving) and search a chest for the totem, then return it to Kangai Mau in Brimhaven.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Must beat level-50 Sigmund with melee or magic. Follow Kazgar through hole under Lumbridge kitchen to Mistag, bring 2 sets of H.A.M. robes to Zanik in Lumbridge cellar (ironmen: pickpocket H.A.M. members instead, better odds during quest). Zanik follows you on a Lumbridge town tour (talk to Cook/Duke/Hans/random Man-or-Woman/Bob/Father Aereck/Lumbridge Guide/a goblin/shopkeeper) then infiltrate the H.A.M. Hideout in disguise (full robes), talk Johanhus, find a hidden trapdoor, and use a multi-step guard-sniping stealth sequence: position Zanik out of guard sightlines, lure each guard into her line of fire one at a time (4 guards total) using wait/say-now dialogue commands. Get arrested by a senior guard, pick the cell lock to escape, find Zanik dead and take her body. Travel to Juna in the Chasm of Tears (via Tears of Guthix path from Lumbridge kitchen cellar - mine a blockage, hop a stone between waterfalls, or games-necklace teleport if that quest is done) and collect 20 tears to revive Zanik in a cutscene. Return to the castle cellar, go to the chicken farm near the water wheel, hide Zanik in an empty crate, descend a trapdoor in full H.A.M. robes to fight and kill 3 Senior Guards (safespottable via Zanik) then Sigmund (level 50, uses protect-missiles in phase 2, safespottable behind boxes). Smash the drilling machine and exit south. Directly extends the H.A.M. Hideout thieving spot with post-quest loot-chest access.

steps.detail: Myreque chain quest 1: deliver steel weapons from Cyreg Paddlehorn's boat at Mort'ton to the Myreque hideout, answer a lore quiz (spelling-checked) to get in, hand over weapons, then safespot-kill a level 97 Skeleton Hellhound before talking your way out. Vanstrom Klause at the tavern is later revealed as the villain -- first appearance in the chain.

steps.detail: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 172 Black demon (safespottable). Talk to King Shareen for a translation book and bark sample, deliver the sample to Hazelmere on the island east of Yanille (fairy ring CLS) for a message. Report back and tell the king about the man with the King's seal and the Daconia rocks; talk to imprisoned Charlie upstairs, search Glough's house cupboard for his journal, confront Glough (he arrests you), get the shipyard password 'Ka-Lu-Min' from Charlie, and escape via the gnome glider. On Karamja: run past level 53 jogres to the Ship Yard, give the password, get a lumber order from the Foreman (fight him or answer his security questions), and deliver the order to the king. Talk to Charlie again, get a key from Anita north-west of the tree, take invasion plans from Glough's chest, deliver them for 4 sticks, spell TUZO on the watchtower pillars in Glough's house, then descend to fight a level 172 Black demon (lure it against the daconia rocks north of the ladder for a safespot). Find the last daconia rock by searching roots, then finish by talking to the king. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Eyes of Glouphrie.

steps.detail: Investigate a theft from the Fortis Grand Museum. Inspect the empty display case, talk to Curator Herminius, then question museum visitors until one mentions a suspicious man. Follow the trail: Regulus Cento by the quetzals, then a Trader Crewmember (do Artima a sail-repair favour first to get his help), then Trader Stan/crew at Port Sarim, then Betty at the Port Sarim rune shop for a note. Head to Varrock Museum, ask Curator Haig Halen about Xerna's Diadem, pickpocket him for the storeroom key, search the crates in the NE storeroom, then confront and shame Haig Halen with the correct dialogue picks (the in-depth guide lists the exact options). Return to Herminius at the Fortis museum to close the case.

steps.detail: Needs Troll Stronghold + Death Plateau + 28 Agility (boostable), plus the ability to beat level 113 Arrg. Play matchmaker for the troll Ug: build a sled with blacksmith Dunstan in Burthorpe out of an iron bar and wax (swamp tar + bucket of wax), sled down a mountain course north-west of Trollheim while dodging ice trolls, stop mid-run to collect rare Trollweiss flowers, then return to Ug and fight Arrg -- he opens with an immediate melee+ranged combo hitting in the 30s and can despawn if not killed fast enough, so gear up before talking to him.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Second Sailing-skill quest. Steve Beanie asks you to fetch a crate of pirate looty from the Port Sarim ledger table, sail it back to The Pandemonium in your cargo hold, and deliver it. He then wants a special key -- take steel bar, hammer and a redberry pie to Thurgo (hut south of Port Sarim) who forges a crowbar. Test the crowbar on a sealed crate found by sailing to the north-west island off The Pandemonium; pry it open from your vessel for a bottle of fish bladder stout, drink it (spawns an optional level 14 drink troll, skippable by returning to the buoys first or disembarking), then return to Steve and open the crate behind him to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Thormac's three rare Kharid Scorpions have escaped; he gives a scorpion cage. Talk to a Seer in Seers' Village to unlock the hidden room in Taverley Dungeon holding scorpion 1 -- reach it past blue dragons (anti-dragon shield recommended) and black demons/poison spiders, using a dusty key from the jailer (or the 70/80 Agility shortcuts to skip). Scorpion 2 is upstairs in Edgeville Monastery (needs Prayer 31 to enter, one-time). Scorpion 3 is in the Barbarian Outpost Agility Course (needs Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl completed). Catch each with the cage, then return the full cage to Thormac.

steps.detail: Fix the lighthouse's light mechanism (tar/tinderbox/glass) then break into its basement past a rune-and-weapon-sacrifice wall puzzle to find the missing keeper Jossik. Kill a level-100 Dagannoth (safespottable), then the level-100 Dagannoth Mother who cycles her damage-type weakness every 18s through all 4 elemental spell schools plus ranged/melee -- bring every attack style.

steps.detail: Second Elemental Workshop-series quest. Find a beaten book with a scroll in the Exam Centre library south of the Digsite, read both, then head to the Elemental Workshop (same entry as EW1). Search machinery in the water elemental room for a hatch key (must have read the scroll first), and smelt 2-3 elemental ore into elemental metal bars beforehand. Downstairs, collect crane and lever schematics, craft a crane claw from one bar, connect a junction box's pipes per the lever schematic's clue, and find 3 randomly-placed cogs + a pipe piece in crates to fix a broken pipe and set cog sizes on the wind tunnel pins (small upper-left, medium lower-left, large right). Run each elemental metal bar through the crane-lava-press-water tank-fan sequence via a cart-advancing lever to produce a primed bar (do this twice for a Mind shield). One level further down, drain a bar into a primed mind bar at the extractor gun/hat (temporarily lowers Magic by 20 per bar powered). With the beaten book in inventory, smith the primed bar into a mind helmet to finish; a second powered bar plus the slashed book from Elemental Workshop I in inventory instead makes a mind shield.

steps.detail: Pickpocket the Zealot's key, descend through the Abandoned Mine's cave levels, solve a lever/points puzzle to route a minecart carrying a light-giving glowing fungus, retrieve the fungus, open a water valve to access a lift, then fight Treus Dayth (level 95 but notably harder than the level suggests -- ranged/magic + Protect from Missiles recommended, not a safe death fight since the fungus is lost on death) before cutting a crystal outcrop to finish.

steps.detail: Not an official miniquest -- the page itself says it does not appear in the quest list; wiki classifies it as one-off 'training or trade unlock' content, not a Quest List miniquest. Three independent errand branches, doable in any order, each separately unlocking a piece of Ali's shop: (1) Runes -- solve/force/pick two lockable rune caskets (small casket allows any method, large casket ONLY the runedoku puzzle); (2) Blackjacks -- talk to the Blackjack seller in Pollnivneach, pick offensive (take 2 hits) or defensive (bribe an urchin) style; (3) Clothes -- give Siamun in Sophanem 3 dyes or 3 matching pelts. All three done = full access to Ali's Discount Wares.

First Temple Knight recruitment quest; needs 32 quest points plus Recruitment Drive (itself needing Black Knights' Fortress + Druidic Ritual), The Lost Tribe (needing Rune Mysteries + Goblin Diplomacy), Priest in Peril, and Enter the Abyss. Get a Commorb device from Tiffy/Sir Amik, meet informant Lord Daquarius in the Black Knights' Base inside Taverley Dungeon after killing a Black Knight, hand 20 essence to the Mage of Zamorak at the Chaos Altar, then use the Commorb to 'scan' and chase fugitive Solus Dellagar across roughly 7 wiki-listed locations in sequence (Rellekka, Musa Point, Wizards' Tower, Dorgesh-Kaan, Ardougne, Champions' Guild, etc. -- exact set/order varies) -- this leg alone can take 20-30+ minutes with no way to skip Savant's cutscene dialogue. Finish with an instanced kill of Solus in the rune essence mine.

steps.detail: A long fetch-quest chain of favours-for-favours across the map; also requires killing Slagilith (level 92, 67% damage reduction unless a pickaxe is equipped or magic used) and a group of dwarf gang members (levels 44/48/49, multicombat). Agreeing phase: Yanni Salika -> Jungle forester (blunt axe) -> Brian in Port Sarim (sharpen axe) -> Aggie in Draynor -> Johanhus Ulsbrecht in the H.A.M. Hideout -> Fred the Farmer -> Seth Groats (chicken farm) -> Horvik in Varrock (give 3 steel bars) -> the Apothecary -> Tassie Slipcast (Barbarian Village) -> Hammerspike Stoutbeard (Dwarven Mine) -> brew a Guthix rest tea for Sanfew (Taverley herblore shop) -> Captain Bleemadge (White Wolf Mountain gnome glider) -> Arhein (Catherby docks) -> Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight (Seers' Village) -> search a sculpture in the Goblin Cave SE of the Fishing Guild -> Wizard Cromperty (NE Ardougne) -> Tindel Marchant (Port Khazard) -> Rantz (Feldip Hills, mattress) -> Gnormadium Avlafrim (cut/replace gems in landing lights, crushed gems cost 500gp each to replace, up to 2). Completing phase: report back to Gnormadium and Rantz; give Tindel the mattress; give Cromperty iron oxide; use an animate rock scroll on the sculpture to fight and kill Slagilith, free Petra, and finish her dialogue; repair Phantuwti's weather vane (3 parts smithed on an anvil with bronze/iron/steel bars) and deliver the forecast to Arhein then Bleemadge then Sanfew; kill Hammerspike's 3 gang members then make a pot lid (via Tassie's clay) for the Apothecary; deliver pigeon cages via Horvik, Seth Groats, Johanhus, Aggie, Brian, then the Jungle forester; finally return to Yanni Salika with the sharpened-axe red mahogany to finish.
Chickens and cows with shortbow. Cheap feathered arrows.

hints.note: Crabs are xp-only; no meaningful loot policy.

steps.detail: One-way point of no return dungeon crawl west of Ardougne toward Tirannwn. Talk King Lathas then Koftik at West Ardougne cave entrance, enter dungeon (no going back except via lever/rope pull). Light an arrow via Koftik's cloth+tinderbox, shoot the bridge guide-rope, cross grid maze carefully (keep HP>15), pull lever. Collect 4 light orbs (avoid wall traps, use plank on flat rocks, disarm trap on 4th), fuel furnace, descend well. Navigate slave-cage/pipe area (50 Thieving shortcut optional). Get unicorn horn via railing-on-boulder trick, kill 3 paladins (safespottable) for badges, use badges+horn on well to open door. Meet dwarves Niloof and Klank (get gauntlets - keep inv slot free), steal Witch's cat to enter her house for loot, kill 3 demons for amulets, brew dwarf beer + tinderbox on tomb. Kill Kalrag the spider (safespottable, ignores Protect Melee), get Iban's dove wearing Klank's gauntlets. Kill a Disciple of Iban for ceremonial robes (required to enter temple), use Doll of Iban on the well in Iban's temple (repeat through his attacks, ~8dmg unblockable spikes) to destroy him. Return to King Lathas via Koftik. Gates access to Tirannwn/Elf lands questline infrastructure.

steps.detail: Fetch-quest structure: give the professor 3 planks, a bronze bar, and molten glass (3 separate hand-ins, must fully finish each before continuing), then retrieve a stolen lens mould from the goblin-infested Observatory Dungeon below (goblin kitchen key from a chest, a sleeping guard to bypass/kill), craft the new lens from the mould + molten glass, and view the rebuilt telescope to receive your (fixed, account-locked) constellation reward. An unlit antipoison spawns near the dungeon entrance if you didn't bring one.
Air Bolt and low alchemy. Unlocks Camelot Teleport.

steps.detail: Normal logs from any tree. Light fires in a row.

Search the shelf in the ruined SW house of Mort'ton and read the diary found there to start (this also unlocks making Serum 207). Make 2 doses of Serum 207 (clean tarromin + vial of water, then add ashes), use one on Razmire (afflicted) in the general store, kill 5 loar shades for their remains, return the remains to Razmire. Buy an olive oil from his general store and 5 timber beams/5 limestone bricks/25 swamp paste (+ optional Flamtaer hammer) from his builders' store. Serum the sick Ulsquire Shauncy east of the store and talk to him twice about the remains/temple. Head to the temple NE of Mort'ton and repair it to 100% with at least 20 sanctity (minigame, safer with other players present), light the sacred altar (or wait for someone with 5+ Firemaking), pour olive oil on the flame for sacred oil, and dip Serum 207 in the flame for permanent-cure Serum 208. Make a pyre log with the sacred oil, take it plus loar remains to one of the stone funeral pyre groups west of the general store, light the pyre (25 Prayer xp), collect the reward, then cure both Razmire and Ulsquire with Serum 208 to finish.

Short intro quest gating Guardians of the Rift. Get the eye amulet from Wizard Persten, use it on the Mage of Zamorak at Varrock's Altar of Chaos, buy (don't steal, or a 10-minute lockout) a strong tea from the Tea Seller to bribe him into an Abyss teleport, then use the amulet on the Dark Mage and solve a colour-matching rift puzzle (touch runic colours until all are white; order is random per player) to receive an abyssal incantation. Return to Persten, teleport to the Wizards' Tower, hand the incantation to Archmage Sedridor, then solve three apprentice riddles with Traiborn (the answer is 11). Finish with a guided Guardians of the Rift tutorial: assemble/charge guardians, mine and grind guardian fragments into essence, and power the Great Guardian at the Mind and Water altars.
